LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY DEFENDANT TERRANCE C. HEATH SHOULD NOT BE HELD IN CONTEMPT OF THIS COURT'S TRO DATED NOVEMBER 13, 2014,IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT IS IN CONTEMPT OF THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER ISSUED ON NOVEMBER 13, 2014. THE COURT ORDERS DEFENDANT IMMEDIATELY TO PROVIDE ACCESS TO PLAINTIFF TO THE PARTNERSHIP'S BUSINESS RECORDS. THE COURT FINES DEFENDANT $900 THAT IS PAYABLE TO THE COURT BY FEBRUARY 17, 2015. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS FOR COMMENCING THIS PROCEEDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $14,413.60 TO BE PAID BY FEBRUARY 17, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/AJR

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED FROM DEC-17-2014 TO JAN-09-2015, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302, TO BE HEARD AT THE SAME TIME AS THE CONTEMPT HEARING. THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER REMAINS IN EFFECT. ACCORDING TO THE COURT'S REGISTER OF ACTIONS, IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT FILE A FORMAL OPPOSITION TO THE OSC RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT IS THEREFORE INCLINED TO GRANT THE APPLICATION. IF DEFENDANT IS IN FACT OPPOSING THE APPLICATION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, THE DEFENDANT SHALL IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSING PAPERS WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE PER SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY DEFENDANT TERRANCE C. HEATH SHOULD NOT BE HELD IN CONTEMPT OF THIS COURT'S TRO DATED NOVEMBER 13, 2014, IS CONTINUED FROM DEC-17-2014 TO JAN-09-2015, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302. THE MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF ITS MOVING/EX PARTE PAPERS FILED ON NOVEMBER 24, 2014, IN COMPLIANCE WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLEASE ATTACH A COVER LETTER TO THOSE PAPERS THAT REFLECT THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT HAS A COURTESY COPY OF DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION TO THE OSC RE CONTEMPT FILED ON DECEMBER 4, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2015 Set For Trial On Mar-07-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Terrance C. Heath Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of This Court'S Tro Dated November 13, 2014
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. HANK BURGOYNE OF THE BURGOYNE LAW GROUP, TEL.NO. 795-4070 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DWIGHT COOPER, TEL. NO. 610-6909 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Terrance C. Heath Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of This Court's Tro Dated November 13, 2014 is Granted. The court finds that defendant is in contempt of the temporary restraining order issued on November 13, 2014. Defendant used partnership assets for purposes other than winding up the law firm and failed to provide access to partnership records. The court orders defendant immediately to provide access to plaintiff to the partnership's business records. The court fines defendant $900 that is payable to the court by February 17, 2015. Plaintiff is awarded reasonable attorney's fees and costs for commencing this proceeding in the amount of $14,413.60 to be paid by February 17, 2015. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/AJR

Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Terrance C. Heath Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of This Court's TRO Dated November 13, 2014 is continued to January 14, 2015 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to be heard by Judge Robertson. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. HANK BURGOYNE of Burgoyne Law Group, Tel No. (415) 795-4070 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. DWIGHT COOPER, Tel No. (415) 610-6909 appearing for the Defendant Terrance C. Heath;
Defendant Terrance C. Heath present.

RULING - The Court required parties to appear. The Court informed counsel at the hearing of its tentative ruling. The matter was argued by counsel. The court adopted its tenative ruling. PLAINTIFF ERIK WS NEWTON'S REQUEST FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the break contract and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action. Plaintiff's declaration demonstrates that Defendant has been using the partnership assests as his own and refused to provide meaningful access to the partnership books. Plaintiff's declaration also includes cancelled checks that show that Defendant has been paying himself from partnership funds. Plaintiff also provides a declaration from Sarah Arnott, an attorney that used to work for the partnership. She substantiates Plaintiff's claims. Equitable relief is appropriate to prevent the further dissipation of partnership assets. The Court orders that Plaintiff post a $25,000.00 bond. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

Order To Show Cause Why Defendant Terrance C. Heath Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of This Court'S Tro Dated November 13, 2014
Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Continued To Jan-09-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The Court continues the OSC re contempt to January 9, 2015. The moving party is ordered to provide a courtesy copy of its moving/ex parte papers filed on November 24, 2014, in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Please attach a cover letter to those papers that reflect the new hearing date. The court has a courtesy copy of defendant's opposition to the OSC re contempt filed on December 4, 2014. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-17-2014 Continued To Jan-09-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. The Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is continued to January 9, 2015, to be heard at the same time as the contempt hearing. The temporary restraining order remains in effect. According to the court's register of actions, it appears that defendant did not file a formal opposition to the OSC re preliminary injunction. The court is therefore inclined to grant the application. If defendant is in fact opposing the application for preliminary injunction, the defendant shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the opposing papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
